commands.repair_reason: This is a protected-label bug-fix PR with sufficient real behavior proof and no blocking findings, so it should go through normal maintainer review and CI rather than ClawSweeper repair.; Cleared: The diff removes an external Dockerfile frontend pull and adds a read-only test, with no new dependency, workflow, secret, or code-execution surface. commands.review_summary:
This PR removes the root Dockerfile `# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1.7` directive and adds a Dockerfile regression test preventing an external frontend parser directive from returning.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Source inspection gives a clear path: current `main` has the external Dockerfile frontend directive and the setup script builds that root Dockerfile with BuildKit; I did not rerun the reporter's Windows 11 + WSL host or force the Docker Hub timeout locally.
commands.review_followup: This is a protected-label bug-fix PR with sufficient real behavior proof and no blocking findings, so it should go through normal maintainer review and CI rather than ClawSweeper repair.
